name: Mixed Berry Tartlets with Lemon Curd
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp red currant jelly, melted with 1 tbsp water
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Lightly spray six 4-inch tartlet pans with cooking spray.
3. Line the pans with wonton wrappers. Trim excess dough at the tops.
4. Lightly spray the insides of the shells.
5. Bake until crisp and golden brown, about 6-8 minutes. Remove shells from pans; cool on a cake rack.
6. Filling: In a medium bowl, whisk together 1/2 cup sugar and the cornstarch. Whisk in the egg.
7. In a small saucepan, bring to a boil the lemon juice, zest, and 1/2 cup sugar. Whisk this into a thin stream into the egg mixture. Return the mixture to the pan and bring to a boil, whisking steadily. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 1 minute or until thickened.
8. Transfer to a medium bowl and cover with plastic wrap, pressing the wrap against the curd to prevent skin from forming. Slit the top slightly with a sharp knife to release steam. Cool to room temperature.
9. Rinse berries. Pat dry. (The recipe can be prepared ahead to this stage.)
10. Spoon 1/4 cup cooled lemon curd into the cooled shells. Arrange berries atop the curd. Brush with jelly. Serve immediately.
